Iran nuclear deal talks resume in Vienna

6 May 2026 By James Smith, BBC Diplomatic Correspondent

Diplomats from Iran and world powers have gathered in Vienna for a new round of negotiations over Tehran's nuclear programme.

The talks, which began on Monday morning, are seen as a crucial opportunity to revive the 2015 nuclear deal that has been on life support since the US withdrawal in 2018.

A senior European diplomat said there was "cautious optimism" about the prospects for progress, but warned that significant gaps remain between the parties.

Iran has consistently demanded that all sanctions be lifted as part of any agreement, while Western powers want Tehran to roll back its nuclear enrichment activities.

The negotiations are expected to last several days, with working groups focusing on technical aspects of verification and sanctions relief.

Important talks underway
